title: Dapr sidecar-injector doesn't clean up Kubernetes Service resources
body:
<!-- If you need to report a security issue please visit https://docs.dapr.io/operations/support/support-security-issues -->

## In what area(s)?


/area runtime


## What version of Dapr?

<!-- Delete all but your choice -->

> 1.13.x


## Expected Behavior

When a Dapr-annotated Kubernetes resource like a `Deployment` get the Dapr sidecar injected, a Kubernetes Service is created for Service to Service invocation to work. 
If the Dapr annotations are removed from the Deployment resource, the pod restart and the Dapr sidecar is not injected, but the Kubernetes Service that was created is never deleted. 

The expected behavior, from a user perspective, is that if the Deployment is not Dapr-enabled, a Service shouldn't be created (or the service should be cleaned up), if there is no Dapr sidecar for that Deployment. 


## Actual Behavior

The Kubernetes Service for Dapr-enabled applications is never cleaned up. If you try to delete the Service manually, the sidecar-injector will recreate the resource. Unless (thanks @alicejgibbons ) you restart the sidecar-injector pod that lives in the Dapr control plane. 


## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

<!-- How can a maintainer reproduce this issue (be detailed) -->
1) Create a Deployment and add Dapr annotations, so the sidecar gets injected
2) Check that a Kubernetes Service was created by Dapr
3) Remove the Dapr annotations from the Deployment and check if the Service is still there.. (it will be there)
4) Try removing the Service manually with `kubectl delete svc ...`
5) Try deleting the sidecar-injector pod inside the `dapr-system` namespace. Now you can delete the service. 

## Release Note

<!-- How should the fix for this issue be communicated in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body:
>  Unless (thanks @alicejgibbons ) you restart the sidecar-injector pod that lives in the Dapr control plane.

Correction: It was the restart of the operator service that refreshes this and then the operator won't force a recreate of the service for the dapr app that is no longer annotated. The service will not be automatically deleted by the operator, but can be manually in this case.
